name: From Sa Pobla to Cap des Pinar - circular route through the Natural Park of s'Albufera de Mallorca
name_original: De Sa Pobla a Cap des Pinar — circular por el Parque Natural de s'Albufera de Mallorca
region_intro: Road cycling around Sa Pobla offers diverse terrain, from flat agricultural lands to challenging mountain ascents. The region is characterized by its proximity to the S'Albufera Natural Park, the foothills of the Serra de Tramuntana mountains, and fertile farmlands. These varied landscapes provide a range of road cycling experiences for different skill levels. Best road cycling routes around Sa Pobla The most popular road cycling route is From Sa Pobla to Cap des Pinar - circular route through the Natural Park of s'Albufera de Mallorca, a 34.5 miles (55.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 27 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the protected wetlands and coastal areas. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Sa Pobla to Algaida - from east to west of Mallorca — return through Es Pla, a moderate 46.9 miles (75.5 km) path. This route traverses the island's interior, showcasing agricultural landscapes and traditional villages. Local road cyclists also love the Coll dels Reis (682 m) – Sa Calobra Road (MA-2141) loop from sa Pobla, a 65.9 miles (106.1 km) trail leading through the Serra de Tramuntana mountains, often completed in about 5 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling around Sa Pobla is defined by flat agricultural areas, coastal roads, and challenging mountain climbs in the Serra de Tramuntana. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides through natural parks to difficult ascents. The routes in Sa Pobla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 10000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sa Pobla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Sa Pobla?
A: Sa Pobla offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 80 different tours available. These range from easy rides through flat agricultural lands to very challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Sa Pobla?
A: The region around Sa Pobla provides diverse terrain. You'll find flat agricultural lands, particularly in the island's interior, and coastal roads. For more challenging rides, routes venture into the foothills and higher elevations of the Serra de Tramuntana mountains, offering significant climbs.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Sa Pobla?
A: Yes, Sa Pobla has easy to moderate routes ideal for beginners or families. Options include circular routes through the S'Albufera Natural Park or flatter rides across the island's interior, often traversing agricultural landscapes. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003184406/from-sa-pobla-to-cap-des-pinar-circular-route-through-the-natural-park-of-s-albufera-de-mallorca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;circular route from Sa Pobla to Cap des Pinar&lt;/a&gt;, which explores protected wetlands.

Q: Where can I find challenging mountain ascents for road cycling near Sa Pobla?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking challenging ascents, routes venture into the Serra de Tramuntana mountains. Iconic climbs like the Coll dels Reis (Sa Calobra Road) and ascents to Puig Major are accessible from Sa Pobla. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll dels Reis (682 m) – Sa Calobra Road (MA-2141) loop from Sa Pobla&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult option.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Sa Pobla offer views of diverse natural features. Many routes pass through or border the S'Albufera Natural Park, a protected wetland. You can also see the fertile farmlands and traditional windmills that give the area its 'land of a thousand windmills' moniker. The majestic Serra de Tramuntana mountains provide a dramatic backdrop for many rides. Additionally, you might encounter highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3727954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrente de s'Albufereta&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/552979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont dels Anglesos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Sa Pobla?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Sa Pobla are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003184406/from-sa-pobla-to-cap-des-pinar-circular-route-through-the-natural-park-of-s-albufera-de-mallorca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Sa Pobla to Cap des Pinar&lt;/a&gt; route is a circular option exploring the natural park. Another challenging loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39564447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll dels Reis (682 m) – Sa Calobra Road (MA-2141) loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Sa Pobla?
A: Sa Pobla is an excellent destination for road cycling for much of the year. The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant weather with moderate temperatures, making them ideal for longer rides. Winter can also be suitable, especially for those looking to escape colder climates, though some mountain passes might be affected by weather.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Sa Pobla?
A: The road cycling routes around Sa Pobla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from the peaceful agricultural landscapes to the challenging mountain climbs, and the well-suited roads for cycling.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for cycling in certain areas, like natural parks?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for road cycling on public roads and designated cycling paths in Mallorca. However, if you plan to venture off-road or into very sensitive natural areas, it's always advisable to check local regulations, especially concerning protected zones like the S'Albufera Natural Park, to ensure compliance.

Q: Can I find bike-friendly amenities like cafes or pubs in Sa Pobla?
A: Sa Pobla and the surrounding towns are well-adapted for cyclists. You'll find numerous bike-friendly cafes and restaurants, particularly in the town center and along popular routes, where you can refuel. Many establishments cater to cyclists, offering places to secure bikes and often serving local specialties.

Q: What are some notable historical sites or towns accessible by road bike from Sa Pobla?
A: From Sa Pobla, you can easily cycle to several charming historical towns and sites. The medieval towns of Pollença and Alcúdia, with their ancient walls and historic centers, are within cycling distance. Within Sa Pobla itself, you can explore the 17th-century Sant Antoni Abat church and the Can Planes Museum of Contemporary Art. The &lt;a href="https://www.visitsapobla.com/en/what-to-do-in-sa-pobla/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sa Pobla Old Town&lt;/a&gt; also offers a glimpse into the island's heritage.

Q: What are the typical distances and elevation gains for road cycling routes in the region?
A: Road cycling routes from Sa Pobla vary significantly in distance and elevation. Easy to moderate routes, like those through the S'Albufera Natural Park, might cover 50-70 km with elevation gains under 500 meters. More challenging routes, such as those into the Serra de Tramuntana, can extend over 100 km with elevation gains exceeding 2000 meters, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33348386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Puig Major – Deià loop&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Always worth a visit, nice place with small cafes and tapas bars
A nice, quiet, pleasant route to cycle without any significant traffic.
In the 19th century, a series of windmills aimed at extracting groundwater were built in Mallorca. Today it is one of the most common ways to extract water from underground wells for crops.

Playa de Muro is a fantastic beach with white sand and turquoise waters that is an epicenter for cycling on the coast. It is the starting point and arrival point of numerous routes that run through the interior of the island due to the concentration of bicycle rental, sale and repair centers and services in its surroundings.
Shade is very welcome, especially in the summer months.
Great road with even better panorama and a few meters of altitude. Unfortunately you can not reach the lighthouse - military restricted area ...

At the end of the highway on the peninsula Victoria stands the old monastery Ermita de la Victoria. On the plaza in the shadow of the huge pine trees you have a great view of the bay of Pollença and the peninsula Formentor. The former convent now houses simple and very nice hotel rooms. From the parking lot at the monastery start various hikes across the peninsula.
Rough stones, so walking barefoot is painful, but this bay is just perfect for a quick jump into the sea.
A beautiful, varied and low-traffic route. You enjoy cycling there.
